The '''Moons of Peril''' are three [[nagua]] imprisoned in [[Neypotzli]], a prison located beneath [[Cam Torum]]. Aimed at mid-level players of at least level 75 combat, the Moons of Peril is heavily inspired by the [[Barrows]]. 

During the time of the [[Old Ones]], the Moons of Peril were summoned by a powerful shaman facing imminent death, and wished to inflict the same on others. Her companions disapproved, and created Neypotzli to imprison her and the three nagua, and summoned [[Eyatlalli]], a nagua of earth and stream, to keep them in check.

The Moons of Peril are popular for mid-level players due to their fairly generous resource rewards (especially for ironmen) and the armour sets. In particular, the blood moon set gives valuable melee strength bonus, serving as a stepping-stone towards [[Bandos armour]]. Since the area gives out free food and powerful potions that can only be used within the challenge, it is all pure profit. The only time the player will ever lose money from it is if they die and must pay to get their belongings back from their gravestone.

Fighting the Moons of Peril requires having completed the [[Perilous Moons]] quest, which acts as an introduction to Neypotzli and the bosses.

==Fight overview==
{{main|Moons of Peril/Strategies}}
The player's trip into [[Neypotzli]] begins in the antechamber, expanding outwards along three accessible entrances. Each of these - the Streambound Cavern, Earthbound Cavern, and the Ancient Prison - have different resources where players can create food and potions that are more powerful than even the best found outside; as such, it is highly recommended to gather supplies from within these caverns rather than bringing in supplies from outside. Campsites around the three areas will also have [[Supply crates (Neypotzli)|supply crates]] that store the required tools to gather resources, and [[cooking stove]]s to cook food and fully restore [[run energy]].

Once the player is ready, they can proceed to face the Moons of Peril; the three bosses share a unique mechanic where their basic attack will hit three times, each dealing more damage than the last; however, if one hit misses, the remainder will as well. This means having good defence bonuses will result in less damage taken overall. [[Protection prayers]] will not do anything against any of their attacks.

While they can be fought by multiple players simultaneously, the boss' health is tied to individual players, meaning each player will effectively have to deal all damage on their own. [[Ironman_Mode|Ironmen]] are thus eligible for full rewards. Additionally, each Moon of Peril has a lower defence bonus against a specific melee style:
* [[Eclipse Moon]] - vulnerable to stab 
* [[Blue Moon]] - vulnerable to crush
* [[Blood Moon]] - vulnerable to slash

==Money making==
{{Mmgsection|Moons of Peril|Killing Blood Moon}}
==Rewards==
{{Main|Lunar Chest}}
Like the Barrows, the rewards players earn are based on how many Moons of Peril are subdued. After defeating at least one moon boss the [[Lunar Chest]] can be looted.

* [[Blood moon armour]] - a set of melee equipment with the ''Bloodrager'' set effect.
* [[Blue moon armour]] - a set of magic equipment with the ''Frostweaver'' set effect.
* [[Eclipse moon armour]] - a set of ranged equipment with the ''Eclipse'' set effect.

[[Eyatlalli]] will accept certain items found inside [[Neypotzli]] in exchange for extra rewards. (''See article:'' [[Eyatlalli]])

Note: Using a piece of the Blood, Blue, or Eclipse moon equipment on Eyatlalli will give the option to sacrifice it for a ~1/50 chance at another equipment roll; players can receive the same item they sacrificed.

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 25 June 2025
|update = Summer Sweep Up: Combat
|change = 
* The clickboxes of the Moons have been adjusted to make them easier to click on mid-combat.
* Fishing and Hunter activities within [[Neypotzli]] have been tweaked to be less buggy.
* The number of tornadoes during the [[Blue Moon]]'s Frost Storm has been reduced.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 2 April 2025
|update = Poll 83: Castle Wars Update
|change = Fixed a bug causing the Moons of Peril to stop functioning if killed with [[burn]] damage.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 15 May 2024
|update = Further Project Rebalance (Skilling) & Varlamore Changes
|change = [[Eyatlalli]]'s dialogue when leaving a moon fight no longer creates disruptive chat boxes.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 17 April 2024
|update = Undead Pirates Tweaks, Varlamore CAs & More
|change = [[Combat Achievements]] have been added for the Moons of Peril.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 3 April 2024
|update = Varlamore Tweaks & Drop Rates
|change = * The [[Eclipse Moon]] will now take damage normally at low health, and players should no longer deal 1 damage regularly when she is at low health.
** ''Players will still regularly deal 1 damage when she is at low health.''
* The [[Eclipse Moon]] can now be consistently killed during the Clone phase.
* The [[ring of recoil]] now correctly loses charges against the bosses.
* Quick-escape is now the default option for the stairs in all three boss chambers.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 27 March 2024
|update = Easter & Varlamore Updates
|poll = 
|change = * The Eclipse Moon's [[flat armour]] has been increased from 4 to 6.
* The Blue Moon now builds up stacks of cold twice as fast, and heals more consistent during its Frost Storm attack.
* The Blood Moon now heals less from its third hit of its standard attack, and heals more from a blood jaguar's hit.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
* The Moons of Peril was originally conceived from an unshared project [[Mod West]] and [[Mod Skylark]] made as part of [[Game Jam]].
* Within 36 hours of release, players spent {{Coins|7967123490}} reclaiming items from their gravestone.{{CiteTwitter|author=Old School Runescape|url=https://twitter.com/OldSchoolRS/status/1771188690557034834|date=22 March 2024|archiveurl=https://archive.is/w1GNw|archivedate=23 March 2024}}
** Within a week of release, players spent {{Coins|11382239258}} reclaiming items from their gravestone.
